title: Train Logistic Regression with Gradient Descent

pytorch_test_cases.test:
import torch
coeffs, losses = train_logreg(torch.tensor([[1.0, 0.5], [-0.5, -1.5], [2.0, 1.5], [-2.0, -1.0]]), torch.tensor([1, 0, 1, 0]), 0.01, 20)
print(len(coeffs), len(losses))

import torch
coeffs, losses = train_logreg(torch.tensor([[1.0], [-1.0], [2.0], [-2.0]]), torch.tensor([1, 0, 1, 0]), 0.1, 5)
print(all(losses[i] >= losses[i+1] for i in range(len(losses)-1)))

tinygrad_test_cases.test:
import numpy as np
# Small dataset test
coeffs, losses = train_logreg(np.array([[1.0, 0.5], [-0.5, -1.5], [2.0, 1.5], [-2.0, -1.0]]), np.array([1, 0, 1, 0]), 0.01, 20)
print(len(coeffs), len(losses))

import numpy as np
# Verify loss decreases
coeffs, losses = train_logreg(np.array([[1.0], [-1.0], [2.0], [-2.0]]), np.array([1, 0, 1, 0]), 0.1, 5)
print(all(losses[i] >= losses[i+1] for i in range(len(losses)-1)))

import numpy as np
# Verify coefficient count = features + 1 (for bias)
coeffs, losses = train_logreg(np.array([[1, 2, 3], [4, 5, 6]]), np.array([0, 1]), 0.01, 3)
print(len(coeffs))
